Bash Aliases Reference

Comprehensive documentation of custom bash aliases and functions for system administration, development, and daily terminal workflows.

200+ Aliases Syntax Highlighted Collapsible Code

System Setup

Aliases and functions for configuring monitors, window managers, and desktop environment detection.

setup-software-gl

Enables software GL rendering using the Mesa llvmpipe driver. Persists the environment variables in ~/.bashrc and optionally disables X11 compositing for XFWM4.

function --no-persist --no-composite
View Code 
setup_software_gl() {
  local persist=1 composite=1
  while [[ "${1:-}" == --* ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --no-persist)   persist=0; shift ;;
      --no-composite) composite=0; shift ;;
      --help|-h) echo -e "📖 \033[1msetup-software-gl\033[0m [--no-persist] [--no-composite]"; return 0 ;;
      *) echo -e "❌ Unknown flag: $1"; return 1 ;;
    esac
  done
  local vars='export LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1
export GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe
export MESA_GL_VERSION_OVERRIDE=3.3'
  if (( persist )); then
    if ! grep -q "LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE" ~/.bashrc; then
      printf '\n%s\n' "$vars" >> ~/.bashrc
    fi
  fi
  export LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1
  export GALLIUM_DRIVER=llvmpipe
  export MESA_GL_VERSION_OVERRIDE=3.3
}
alias setup-software-gl='setup_software_gl'
Usage Examples
$
$
$

show-all-env-vars

Lists all environment variables ( env ), sorted alphabetically.

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-all-env-vars='env | grep -v "^$" | sort'
Usage Examples
$

show-all-printenv-vars

Lists all exported variables ( printenv ), sorted alphabetically.

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-all-printenv-vars='printenv | grep -v "^$" | sort'
Usage Examples
$

show-all-sh-vars

Lists all shell variables ( set -o posix; set ), sorted alphabetically.

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-all-sh-vars='( set -o posix; set ) | grep -v "^$" | sort'

cat-ssh-service

Displays the SSH systemd service unit file from /lib/systemd/system/ssh.service .

simple alias
View Code 
alias cat-ssh-service='sudo cat /lib/systemd/system/ssh.service 2>/dev/null || echo "SSH service file not found"'

$

show-display-session

Displays the current session type ( x11 or wayland ) from the environment variable $XDG_SESSION_TYPE .

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-display-session="echo \$XDG_SESSION_TYPE"
Usage Examples
$

show-desktop-session

Displays the current desktop session name from $XDG_SESSION_DESKTOP .

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-desktop-session="echo \$XDG_SESSION_DESKTOP"
Usage Examples
$

show-display-manager

Displays the service unit name of the active display manager via systemctl .

simple alias
View Code 
alias show-display-manager='systemctl show -p Id display-manager.service'
Usage Examples
$

show-greeter

Detects the active display manager and shows its greeter configuration . Supports LightDM , GDM3 , SDDM , LXDM , XDM and SLiM .

function
View Code 
show_greeter() {
  local dm
  dm=$(cat /etc/X11/default-display-manager 2>/dev/null | xargs basename)
  if [[ -z "$dm" ]]; then
    dm=$(systemctl status display-manager 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '(?<=Loaded: loaded \().*?(?=;)' | xargs basename)
  fi
  case "$dm" in
    lightdm)
      grep -r "greeter-session" /etc/lightdm/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^#"
      ;;
    gdm3|gdm)
      echo "=== GDM3 Display Manager ==="
      cat /etc/gdm3/daemon.conf 2>/dev/null
      cat /etc/gdm3/custom.conf 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    sddm)
      grep -i "theme\|greeter" /etc/sddm.conf /etc/sddm.conf.d/*.conf 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Unknown DM: '$dm'"
      ;;
  esac
}
alias show-greeter='show_greeter'
Usage Examples
$

show-win-mng

Detects which window manager is running by scanning the process list against 50+ known X11/Wayland WMs including i3, awesome, bspwm, sway, hyprland and more.

function
View Code 
show_win_mng() {
  local WM_PATTERN='openbox|fluxbox|icewm|jwm|fvwm3|compiz|blackbox|metacity|mutter|kwin|xfwm4|i3|awesome|bspwm|herbstluftwm|spectrwm|qtile|river|niri|dwm|xmonad|sway|weston|labwc|wayfire|hyprland|cage|phoc|cosmic-comp'
  ps aux | grep -E "$WM_PATTERN" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $11}' | xargs -I{} basename {}
}
alias show-win-mng='show_win_mng'
Usage Examples
$

Installs GNOME autostart entry that restarts xdg-desktop-portal backends for dark-mode consistency.

View Code

Removes the portal dark autostart desktop entry.

View Code

show-display-server

Show human-readable display server info

function
View Code 
show_display_server() {
  local display="${DISPLAY:-unset}"
  
  case "$display" in
    unset)
      echo "No display server - headless environment or TTY"
      ;;
    :0)
      echo "X11 server on local machine (primary display)"
      ;;
    :0.0)
      echo "X11 server on local machine, screen 0"
      ;;
    :1)
      echo "Second X11 server"
      ;;
    :1.0)
      echo "Second X11 server, screen 0"
      ;;
    :2)
      echo "Third X11 server"
      ;;
    :10|:11|:20|:30|:99)
      echo "Nested X server or virtual display"
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Display: $display (custom or remote X server)"
      ;;
  esac
}
alias show-display-server='show_display_server'
Usage Examples
$

show-greeter-verbose

Show verbose greeter/display-manager configuration

function
View Code 
show_greeter_verbose() {
  local dm
  dm=$(cat /etc/X11/default-display-manager 2>/dev/null | xargs basename)

  if [[ -z "$dm" ]]; then
    dm=$(systemctl status display-manager 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '(?<=Loaded: loaded \().*?(?=;)' | xargs basename)
  fi

  case "$dm" in
    lightdm)
      grep -r "greeter-session" /etc/lightdm/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^#"
      ;;
    gdm3|gdm)
      echo "=== GDM3 Display Manager ==="
      echo "Greeter: GNOME Shell (built-in, no separate process)"
      echo ""
      echo "=== GDM Daemon Config ==="
      cat /etc/gdm3/daemon.conf 2>/dev/null
      echo ""
      echo "=== GDM Custom Config ==="
      cat /etc/gdm3/custom.conf 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    sddm)
      grep -i "theme\|greeter" /etc/sddm.conf /etc/sddm.conf.d/*.conf 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^#"
      ;;
    lxdm)
      grep -i "theme\|greeter" /etc/lxdm/lxdm.conf 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^#"
      ;;
    xdm)
      echo "XDM: built-in greeter, config at /etc/X11/xdm/"
      ls /etc/X11/xdm/ 2>/dev/null
      ;;
    slim)
      grep -i "theme\|greeter" /etc/slim.conf 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^#"
      ;;
    *)
      echo "Unknown or undetected display manager: '$dm'"
      echo "Hint: try 'systemctl status display-manager'"
      ;;
  esac
}
alias show-greeter-verbose='show_greeter_verbose'

net-probe

Probes a network host with ARP , ping , netcat and route query. Full network diagnostic in a single command.

function $1: IP $2: port --gateway --local
View Code 
net_probe() {
  local ip="" port=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --gateway)
        ip=$(ip route | awk '/default/{print $3; exit}')
        shift ;;
      --local)
        ip="192.168.1.1"; port="22"
        shift ;;
      *)
        if [ -z "$ip" ]; then ip="$1"; else port="$1"; fi; shift ;;
    esac
  done
  arp -a 2>/dev/null
  ping -c 4 "$ip"
  [ -n "$port" ] && nc -vz "$ip" "$port"
  ip route get "$ip"
}
alias net-probe='net_probe'
Usage Examples
$
$

ls-tcp-proc-config function

Show /proc/net/tcp, tcp6, and IPv4 TCP config files.

View Code 
ls_tcp_proc_config() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📁 NET PROCESS FILES ──\033[0m\n"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 TCP IPv4 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo TCP IPv4 info\033[0m"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 TCP IPv6 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/tcp6 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo TCP IPv6 info\033[0m"
  sleep 1
  if [[ -d /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf ]]; then
    echo -e "\033[1;34m── ⚙️  TCP CONFIG FILES ──\033[0m\n"
    sudo find /proc/sys/net/ipv4/conf -type f -name "tcp_*" -exec sh -c 'echo -e "\033[1;34m── {} ──\033[0m"; cat "$1" 2>/dev/null; echo ""' _ {} \;
  fi
}
alias ls-tcp-proc-config='ls_tcp_proc_config'

ls-tcp-config-proc

Alias for ls-tcp-proc-config.

alias ls-tcp-config-proc='ls_tcp_proc_config'

ls-net-sockstats function

Show IPv4 and IPv6 socket statistics from /proc/net/sockstat.

View Code 
ls_net_sockstats() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📊 SOCKSTAT INFO ──\033[0m\n"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 SOCKSTAT IPv4 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/sockstat 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo IPv4 sockstat info\033[0m"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 SOCKSTAT IPv6 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/sockstat6 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo IPv6 sockstat info\033[0m"
}
alias ls-net-sockstats='ls_net_sockstats'

ls-sockstats

Alias for ls-net-sockstats.

View Code 
alias ls-sockstats='ls_net_sockstats'

ls-sock-statistics

Alias for ls-net-sockstats.

View Code 
alias ls-sock-statistics='ls_net_sockstats'

ls-net-snmp function

Show SNMP TCP statistics from /proc/net/snmp and snmp6.

View Code 
ls_net_snmp() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📊 SNMP INFO ──\033[0m\n"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 SNMP TCP IPv4 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/snmp 2>/dev/null | grep -A10 "Tcp:" || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo SNMP TCP info\033[0m"
  sleep 1
  echo -e "\033[1;34m── 🌐 SNMP TCP IPv6 ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo cat /proc/net/snmp6 2>/dev/null | grep -A10 "Tcp:" || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo SNMP TCP6 info\033[0m"
}
alias ls-net-snmp='ls_net_snmp'

ls-snmp

Alias for ls-net-snmp.

View Code 
alias ls-snmp='ls_net_snmp'

ls-tcp-iptables function

Show iptables rules filtered for TCP across filter, nat, and raw tables.

View Code 
ls_tcp_iptables_rules() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔒 ALL IPTABLES RULES FOR TCP ──\033[0m\n"
  sleep 3
  sudo iptables -L -n -v --line-numbers 2>/dev/null | grep -i "tcp" || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo iptables rules for TCP or iptables not available\033[0m"
  sleep 2
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔒 NAT IPTABLES RULES FOR TCP ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo iptables -t nat -L -n -v --line-numbers 2>/dev/null | grep -i "tcp" || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo NAT iptables rules for TCP or iptables not available\033[0m"
  sleep 2
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔒 RAW IPTABLES FOR TCP ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo iptables -t raw -L -n -v --line-numbers 2>/dev/null | grep -i "tcp" || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo RAW iptables rules for TCP or iptables not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-tcp-iptables='ls_tcp_iptables_rules'

ls-iptables-tcp

Alias for ls-tcp-iptables.

View Code 
alias ls-iptables-tcp='ls_tcp_iptables_rules'

ls-tcp-iptables-rules

Alias for ls-tcp-iptables.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-iptables-rules='ls_tcp_iptables_rules'

ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control function

Show TCP congestion control algorithm and available algorithms.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4_congestion_control() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── ⚙️  TCP CONGESTION CONTROL CONFIGS ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_available_congestion_control 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_available_congestion_control sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_congestion_control sysctl not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control='ls_sys_tcp_v4_congestion_control'

ls-sysctl-tcp-v4-congestion-control

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control.

View Code 
alias ls-sysctl-tcp-v4-congestion-control='ls_sys_tcp_v4_congestion_control'

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-sysctl-v4-congestion-control='ls_sys_tcp_v4_congestion_control'

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control.

View Code

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-congestion-control.

ls-sys-tcp-v4-time-control function

Show TCP keepalive, timeout, and retry sysctl parameters.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4_time_control() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── ⏱️  TCP TIME CONTROL CONFIGS ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_keepalive_time sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_fin_timeout 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_fin_timeout sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_retries1 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_retries1 sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_retries2 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_retries2 sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_synack_retries 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_synack_retries sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_syn_retries 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_syn_retries sysctl not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-sys-tcp-v4-time-control='ls_sys_tcp_v4_time_control'

ls-sysctl-tcp-v4-time-control

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-time-control.

View Code 
alias ls-sysctl-tcp-v4-time-control='ls_sys_tcp_v4_time_control'

ls-sys-tcp-v4-limits function

Show TCP backlog, orphan, and TIME_WAIT bucket limits.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4_limits() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📏 TCP LIMIT CONFIGS ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_max_syn_backlog sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_max_tw_buckets 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_max_tw_buckets sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_max_orphans 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_max_orphans sysctl not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-sys-tcp-v4-limits='ls_sys_tcp_v4_limits'

ls-sys-tcp-v4-mtu function

Show TCP base MSS and MTU probing sysctl settings.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4_mtu() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📐 TCP MTU CONFIGS ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_base_mss 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_base_mss sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_mtu_probing 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_mtu_probing sysctl not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-sys-tcp-v4-mtu='ls_sys_tcp_v4_mtu'

ls-sysctl-tcp-v4-mtu

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-mtu.

View Code

Alias for ls-sys-tcp-v4-mtu.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-sysctl-v4-mtu='ls_sys_tcp_v4_mtu'

ls-sys-base-v4-config function

Show TCP buffer sizes, fast open, and syncookies sysctl settings.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4_base_config() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔧 BASE TCP CONFIGS ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_rmem 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_rmem sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_wmem 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_wmem sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.core.rmem_default 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mcore rmem_default sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.core.wmem_default 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mcore wmem_default sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.core.rmem_max 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mcore rmem_max sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.core.wmem_max 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mcore wmem_max sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_fastopen 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_fastopen sysctl not available\033[0m"
  sudo sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mtcp_syncookies sysctl not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-sys-base-v4-config='ls_sys_tcp_v4_base_config'

ls-sys-tcp-v4 function

Aggregated view of all IPv4 TCP sysctl settings.

View Code 
ls_sys_tcp_v4() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🌐 SYSCTL CONFIGS FOR IPv4 ──\033[0m\n"
  ls_sys_tcp_v4_congestion_control
  sleep 2
  ls_sys_tcp_v4_time_control
  sleep 2
  ls_sys_tcp_v4_limits
  sleep 2
  ls_sys_tcp_v4_mtu
  sleep 2
  ls_sys_tcp_v4_base_config
}
alias ls-sys-tcp-v4='ls_sys_tcp_v4'

ls-tcp-listening-ports function

Show listening TCP ports via lsof.

View Code 
ls_tcp_listening_ports() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 👂 Listening TCP Ports ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo lsof -i TCP -s TCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo listening TCP ports or lsof not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-tcp-listening-ports='ls_tcp_listening_ports'

ls-tcp-listen-ports

Alias for ls-tcp-listening-ports.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-listen-ports='ls_tcp_listening_ports'

ls-tcp-lp

Alias for ls-tcp-listening-ports.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-lp='ls_tcp_listening_ports'

ls-tcp-established-connections function

Show established TCP connections via lsof.

View Code 
ls_tcp_established_connections() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔗 Established TCP Connections ──\033[0m\n"
  sudo lsof -i TCP -s TCP:ESTABLISHED 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo established TCP connections or lsof not available\033[0m"
}
alias ls-tcp-established-connections='ls_tcp_established_connections'

ls-tcp-established-conns

Alias for ls-tcp-established-connections.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-established-conns='ls_tcp_established_connections'

ls-tcp-est-conns

Alias for ls-tcp-established-connections.

View Code

ls-tcp-est-connections

Alias for ls-tcp-established-connections.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-est-connections='ls_tcp_established_connections'

ls-tcp-active-connections function

Show all active TCP connections, listening ports, and established connections.

View Code 
ls_tcp_active_connections() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 🔌 All Active TCP Connections ──\033[0m\n"
  sleep 3
  sudo lsof -i TCP -nP 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mNo active TCP connections or lsof not available\033[0m"
  sleep 2
  ls_tcp_listening_ports
  sleep 2
  ls_tcp_established_connections
}
alias ls-tcp-active-connections='ls_tcp_active_connections'

ls-tcp-active-conns

Alias for ls-tcp-active-connections.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-active-conns='ls_tcp_active_connections'

ls-tcp-act-conns

Alias for ls-tcp-active-connections.

View Code 
alias ls-tcp-act-conns='ls_tcp_active_connections'

ls-active-tcp-net-sockets function

Show TCP sockets via ss and netstat (with deprecation warning for netstat).

View Code 
ls_active_tcp_net_sockets() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📡 SS OUTPUT ──\033[0m\n"
  if command -v ss &>/dev/null; then
    sudo ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mss command not available\033[0m"
  fi
  sleep 3
  echo -e "\n\033[1;34m── 📡 NETSTAT OUTPUT ──\033[0m\n"
  if command -v netstat &>/dev/null; then
    echo -e "⚠️  \033[1;33mWarning: netstat is deprecated. Consider using ss instead.\033[0m"
    sudo netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null || echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mnetstat command not available\033[0m"
  fi
}
alias ls-active-tcp-net-sockets='ls_active_tcp_net_sockets'

ls-active-tcp-sockets

Alias for ls-active-tcp-net-sockets.

View Code 
alias ls-active-tcp-sockets='ls_active_tcp_net_sockets'

ls-tcp-config function

Full TCP diagnostic: proc files, sockstats, SNMP, iptables, sysctl, connections, and sockets.

View Code 
ls_tcp_config() {
  echo -e "\n\033[1;36m══════════ 🌐 TCP CONFIGURATION ══════════\033[0m\n"
  sleep 2
  ls_tcp_proc_config
  sleep 2
  ls_net_sockstats
  sleep 2
  ls_net_snmp
  sleep 1
  ls_tcp_iptables_rules
  sleep 1
  ls_sys_tcp_v4
  sleep 3
  ls_tcp_active_connections
  sleep 3
  ls_active_tcp_net_sockets
}
alias ls-tcp-config='ls_tcp_config'

Alias for ls-tcp-config.

cat-band

Measures bandwidth usage between start and Enter. Based on the script by Luke Smith . Reads from /sys/class/net/[ew]*/statistics/rx_bytes .

function
View Code 
cat_band() {
  init="$(($(cat /sys/class/net/[ew]*/statistics/rx_bytes | paste -sd '+')))"
  printf "Recording bandwidth.. Press enter to stop."
  read -r "stop"
  fin="$(($(cat /sys/class/net/[ew]*/statistics/rx_bytes | paste -sd '+')))"
  printf "%4sB of bandwidth used.\\n" $(numfmt --to=iec $(($fin-$init)))
}
alias cat-band='cat_band'
Usage Examples
$

track-power-usage

Runs powerstat (RAPL) and saves the output to a timestamped log in ~/.logs/ .

function $1: duration (3600) $2: tick (1)
View Code 
track_power_usage() {
  local duration=${1:-3600}
  local tick=${2:-1}
  if ! command -v powerstat &>/dev/null; then
    echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mpowerstat not found.\033[0m Install with: sudo apt install powerstat"
    return 1
  fi
  mkdir -p "$HOME/.logs"
  local log="$HOME/.logs/powerstat-$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S).log"
  echo -e "⚡ \033[1;36mRecording power stats for ${duration}s (tick: ${tick}s) → $log\033[0m"
  sudo stdbuf -oL powerstat -R "$tick" "$duration" | tee "$log"
}
alias track-power-usage='track_power_usage'
Usage Examples
$

Git Aliases

Shorthand aliases for common Git operations: staging, commits, branches, stash, and more.

ga

Shorthand for git add

View Code 
alias ga='git add'
Usage Examples
$

gal

Add all files: git add .

View Code 
alias gal='git add .'
Usage Examples
$

gc

Shorthand for git commit

View Code 
alias gc='git commit'
Usage Examples
$

gca

Commit all with message: git commit -a -m

View Code 
alias gca='git commit -a -m'
Usage Examples
$

gps

git push

View Code 
alias gps='git push'
Usage Examples
$

gpl

View Code 
alias gpl='git pull'
Usage Examples
$

gps-oh

Push current HEAD to origin: git push origin HEAD

View Code 
alias gps-oh='git push origin HEAD'

gf

git fetch

View Code 
alias gf='git fetch'

grs

git reset

View Code 
alias grs='git reset'
Usage Examples
$

grs-h

Reset hard: git reset --hard

View Code 
alias grs-h='git reset --hard'

grs--1

Undo last commit (keeps changes): git reset HEAD~1

View Code 
alias grs--1='git reset HEAD~1'

grv

git revert

View Code 
alias grv='git revert'
Usage Examples
$

git-tree-info

Show Git work tree info: repo dir, common dir, top level, and superproject status

function
View Code 
alias git-tree-info='git_tree_info'
Usage Examples
$

Utilities

File analysis, backup, hardware shortcuts, and everyday utilities.

wc-l-novendors

Counts total lines in the directory, excluding vendor/ , node_modules/ and .git/ . Displays the running total during execution.

function
View Code 
wc_lines_novendors() {
  local files=0 total=0
  while IFS= read -r -d $'\0' file; do
    lines=$(wc -l < "$file")
    total=$((total+lines))
    files=$((files+1))
    printf "%s %d\n" "$file" "$total"
  done < <(find . -type f \
        ! -path '*/vendor/*' \
        ! -path '*/node_modules/*' \
        ! -path '*/.git/*' \
        -print0)
  echo "-> TOTAL: $total lines in $files files"
}
alias wc-l-novendors='wc_lines_novendors'
Usage Examples
$

hfold

Shows the heaviest folders by size in GB.

function $1: root (.) $2: top N (10)
View Code 
heavy_folders() {
  local root=${1:-.} top=${2:-10}
  du -Sb "$root"/* 2>/dev/null \
    | sort -nr \
    | head -n "$top" \
    | awk '{printf "%.2fGB\t%s\n",$1/1024/1024/1024,$2}'
}
alias hfold='heavy_folders'
Usage Examples
$

fully-randomized-file-names

Rename all files in the current directory to random 16-char alphanumeric names , preserving extensions. Requires sudo .

function
View Code 
alias fully-randomized-file-names='fully_randomized_file_names'

$

simple alias

uri-decode

Decodes a percent-encoded URI string (e.g. %20 → space).

function $1: uri
View Code 
uri_decode() {
  printf '%b' "${1//%/\\x}"
}
alias uri-decode='uri_decode'
Usage Examples
$
$

printf-tr

Printf with field-width, delimiter, and tr -based character substitution.

function $1: delimiter $2: width $3: type $4: target $5: pattern $6: substitute $7: tr_from $8: tr_to
View Code 
printftr() {
  local delimiter="${1:-}"
  local width="${2:-}"
  local type="${3:-%b}"
  local target="${4:?No argument for capturing provided}"
  local pattern="${5:?No pattern to replace provided. Failed.}"
  local substitute="${6:?No replacer given. Failed.}"
  local tr_from="${7:-}"
  local tr_to="${8:- }"
  printf "%${delimiter}${width}${type#%}" "${target//${pattern}/${substitute}}" | tr "$tr_from" "$tr_to"
}
alias printf-tr='printftr'
Usage Examples
$
$
$

cat-indexed

Lists files with index numbers and displays the contents of a file by its 1-based index.

function $1: index
View Code 
cat_indexed() {
  local index="${1:-1}"
  (ls | nl) && file=$(ls | sed -n "${index}p") && output=$(strings "$file") && echo "${output:-"INDEXED HAS NO CONTENT"} 2>/dev/null" || echo "No file found at index $index"
}
alias cat-indexed='cat_indexed'
Usage Examples
$
$

run-cmds

Runs multiple commands against a single target argument sequentially.

function $1: target $@: commands
View Code 
run_cmds() {
  [ -z "$1" ] && { echo "Error: target required" >&2; return 1; }
  local target="$1"
  shift
  for cmd in "$@"; do
    eval "$cmd \"$target\"" 2>/dev/null
  done
}
alias run-cmds='run_cmds'
Usage Examples
$
$

function
View Code 
alias man-fopt='man_fopt'

$
$

calculate-check-sum function

Calculate Modulus N check digits for a numeric string (e.g. CPF mod-11, CNPJ).

View Code 
calculate_check_sum() {
  local state="${1:?Usage: calculate_check_sum <digits> <modulus>}"
  local total="${2:?Usage: calculate_check_sum <digits> <modulus>}"
  # ... validates inputs, iterates positions, computes weighted sum mod total ...
  echo "$state"
}

calc-checksum alias

Alias for calculate-check-sum.

View Code 
alias calc-checksum='calculate_check_sum'

get-group-users function

List users belonging to a specific group.

View Code 
get_group_users() {
  local group_name="${1:?Usage: get_group_users <group_name>}"
  local groups=($(cut -d: -f1 /etc/group))
  if [[ ! " ${groups[*]} " =~ " ${group_name} " ]]; then
    echo "Group '$group_name' does not exist in the system. Aborting."
    return 1
  fi
  grep "^${group_name}:" /etc/group | cut -d: -f4 | tr ',' '\n' | sort -u
}

ls-group-users alias

Alias for get-group-users.

View Code 
alias ls-group-users='get_group_users'

View Code 
alias stringify-user-settings='sudo strings ~/.config/dconf/user'

View Code

System Info Aliases

OOM memory management, earlyoom monitoring, and systemd-oomd utilities for diagnosing out-of-memory events on Linux.

cat-oom-conf

Displays the systemd OOM daemon configuration from /etc/systemd/oomd.conf , or a message if the file is absent.

View Code 
alias cat-oom-conf='sudo cat /etc/systemd/oomd.conf 2>/dev/null || echo "No OOM config file found"'
Usage Examples
$

show-oom-kill-alloc

Shows the vm.oom_kill_allocating_task sysctl value (0 = kill random process, 1 = kill the allocating task).

View Code 
alias show-oom-kill-alloc='sudo sysctl vm.oom_kill_allocating_task'
Usage Examples
$

View Code

follow-early-oom-rec

Follows earlyoom daemon output with verbose reporting at a configurable interval.

function $1: interval (default: 2)
View Code 
follow_early_oom_rec() {
  local interval="${1:-2}"
  earlyoom -vr "$interval"
}
alias follow-early-oom-rec='follow_early_oom_rec'

watch-mem-hogs

Watches memory-hungry processes sorted by RSS in real time using watch and ps aux .

function $1: interval (default: 0.25)
View Code 
watch_mem_hogs() {
  local interval="${1:-0.25}"
  watch -n "$interval" 'ps aux --sort=-%mem | awk "{print \$1,\$2,\$4,\$5,\$6,\$11}"'
}
alias watch-mem-hogs='watch_mem_hogs'
Usage Examples
$

watch-cpu-hogs

Watches CPU-hungry processes sorted by CPU usage in real time using watch and ps aux .

function $1: interval (default: 0.25)
View Code 
watch_cpu_hogs() {
  local interval="${1:-0.25}"
  watch -n "$interval" 'ps aux --sort=-%cpu | awk "{print \$1,\$2,\$3,\$8,\$11}"'
}
alias watch-cpu-hogs='watch_cpu_hogs'
Usage Examples
$

ls-sys-vm-overcommit

Show VM overcommit memory and ratio settings.

function
View Code 
ls_sys_vm_overcommit() {
  printf "[*] Checking vm.overcommit_memory...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.overcommit_memory 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No overcommit_memory info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking vm.overcommit_ratio...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.overcommit_ratio 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No overcommit_ratio info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-vm-overcommit='ls_sys_vm_overcommit'

ls-sys-vm-over-mem

Alias for ls-sys-vm-overcommit.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-vm-over-mem='ls_sys_vm_overcommit'

function
View Code

  printf "[*] Checking vm.oom_kill_allocating_task...\n"

}
alias ls-sys-vm-oom-kill-alloc='ls_sys_vm_oom_kill_alloc'

function
View Code 
ls_sys_vm_swappiness() {

alias ls-sys-vm-swappiness='ls_sys_vm_swappiness'

Alias for ls-sys-vm-swappiness.

View Code

ls-sys-vm-dirty-ratios

Show VM dirty ratio and dirty background ratio.

function
View Code 
ls_sys_vm_dirty_ratios() {
  printf "[*] Checking vm.dirty_ratio...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.dirty_ratio 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No dirty_ratio info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking vm.dirty_background_ratio...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.dirty_background_ratio 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No dirty_background_ratio info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-vm-dirty-ratios='ls_sys_vm_dirty_ratios'

ls-sys-vm-dirtyness

Alias for ls-sys-vm-dirty-ratios.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-vm-dirtyness='ls_sys_vm_dirty_ratios'

ls-sys-kernel-hungs

Show kernel hung task timeout, warnings, and backtrace settings.

function
View Code 
ls_sys_kernel_hungs() {
  printf "[*] Checking for kernel hungs...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_timeout_secs 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task timeout info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking for warning about hung tasks...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_warnings 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task warning info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking for hung task backtraces...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_all_cpu_backtrace 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task backtrace info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-kernel-hungs='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-k-hungs

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-hungs='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-kernel-hung-tasks

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-kernel-hung-tasks='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-k-hung-tasks

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-hung-tasks='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-kernel-schedules

Show kernel scheduler settings (latency, granularity, autogroup, migration cost).

function
View Code 
ls_sys_kernel_schedules() {
  printf "[*] Checking for scheduler info...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_latency_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler latency info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_min_granularity_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler min granularity info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_child_runs_first 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler child runs first info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_autogroup_enabled 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler autogroup info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_migration_cost_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler migration cost info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-kernel-schedules='ls_sys_kernel_schedules'

ls-sys-k-schedules

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-schedules.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-schedules='ls_sys_kernel_schedules'

ls-sys-kernel-info function

Aggregated view of kernel hung-task and scheduler parameters.

View Code 
ls_sys_kernel_info() {
  printf "\n[=== KERNEL INFO ===]\n"
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== KERNEL HUNGS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_kernel_hungs
  sleep 2
  printf "[=== KERNEL SCHEDULER ===]\n"
  ls_sys_kernel_schedules
}

ls-sys-k-info

Short alias for ls-sys-kernel-info.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-info='ls_sys_kernel_info'

ls-sys-vm-info function

Aggregated view of VM overcommit, OOM, swappiness, and dirty-ratio parameters.

View Code 
ls_sys_vm_info() {
  printf "\n[=== VM INFO ===]\n"
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM OVERCOMMIT ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_overcommit
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM OOM KILL ALLOCATING TASK ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_oom_kill_alloc
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM SWAPPINESS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_swappiness
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM DIRTY RATIOS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_dirty_ratios
}

ls-sys-info function

Show all kernel and VM sysctl info (calls ls_sys_kernel_info + ls_sys_vm_info).

View Code 
ls_sys_info() {
  ls_sys_kernel_info
  sleep 3
  ls_sys_vm_info
}

ls-sys-vm-overcommit

Show VM overcommit memory and ratio settings.

function
View Code 
ls_sys_vm_overcommit() {
  printf "[*] Checking vm.overcommit_memory...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.overcommit_memory 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No overcommit_memory info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking vm.overcommit_ratio...\n"
  sudo sysctl vm.overcommit_ratio 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No overcommit_ratio info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-vm-overcommit='ls_sys_vm_overcommit'

ls_sys_kernel_hungs() {

  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_timeout_secs 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task timeout info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking for warning about hung tasks...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_warnings 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task warning info available\n"
  printf "[*] Checking for hung task backtraces...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.hung_task_all_cpu_backtrace 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No hung task backtrace info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-kernel-hungs='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-k-hungs

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-hungs='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-kernel-hung-tasks

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-kernel-hung-tasks='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-k-hung-tasks

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-hungs.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-hung-tasks='ls_sys_kernel_hungs'

ls-sys-kernel-schedules

Show kernel scheduler settings (latency, granularity, autogroup, migration cost).

function
View Code 
ls_sys_kernel_schedules() {
  printf "[*] Checking for scheduler info...\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_latency_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler latency info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_min_granularity_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler min granularity info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_child_runs_first 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler child runs first info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_autogroup_enabled 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler autogroup info available\n"
  sudo sysctl kernel.sched_migration_cost_ns 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No scheduler migration cost info available\n"
}
alias ls-sys-kernel-schedules='ls_sys_kernel_schedules'

ls-sys-k-schedules

Alias for ls-sys-kernel-schedules.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-schedules='ls_sys_kernel_schedules'

ls-sys-kernel-info function

Aggregated view of kernel hung-task and scheduler parameters.

View Code 
ls_sys_kernel_info() {
  printf "\n[=== KERNEL INFO ===]\n"
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== KERNEL HUNGS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_kernel_hungs
  sleep 2
  printf "[=== KERNEL SCHEDULER ===]\n"
  ls_sys_kernel_schedules
}

ls-sys-k-info

Short alias for ls-sys-kernel-info.

View Code 
alias ls-sys-k-info='ls_sys_kernel_info'

ls-sys-vm-info function

Aggregated view of VM overcommit, OOM, swappiness, and dirty-ratio parameters.

View Code 
ls_sys_vm_info() {
  printf "\n[=== VM INFO ===]\n"
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM OVERCOMMIT ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_overcommit
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM OOM KILL ALLOCATING TASK ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_oom_kill_alloc
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM SWAPPINESS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_swappiness
  sleep 1
  printf "[=== VM DIRTY RATIOS ===]\n"
  ls_sys_vm_dirty_ratios
}

ls-sys-info function

Show all kernel and VM sysctl info (calls ls_sys_kernel_info + ls_sys_vm_info).

View Code 
ls_sys_info() {
  ls_sys_kernel_info
  sleep 3
  ls_sys_vm_info
}

find-zombies

Lists zombie processes (state Z ) from ps aux output.

function $1: head (default: 20)
View Code 
find_zombies() {
  local head="${1:-20}"
  ps aux | awk '$8 ~ /Z/ {print}' | head -n "$head"
}
alias find-zombies='find_zombies'
Usage Examples
$

cat-pid-oom-kill-score

Displays the OOM kill score for a process from /proc/<pid>/oom_score . Higher scores are killed first.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat_pid_oom_kill_score() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-kill-score <pid>"}"
  echo ==== "OOM SCORE (TO BE KILLED)" ====
  sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score 2>/dev/null || echo "No OOM score info available"
}
alias cat-pid-oom-kill-score='cat_pid_oom_kill_score'

follow-pid-oom-kill-score

Watches OOM kill score changes for a process in real time.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
follow_pid_oom_kill_score() {
  local pid="${1?\"Usage: follow-pid-oom-kill-score <pid>\"}"
  watch -n 1 "echo ==== \"OOM SCORE (TO BE KILLED)\" ==== && sudo cat /proc/$pid/oom_score 2>/dev/null || echo 'No OOM score info available'"
}
alias follow-pid-oom-kill-score='follow_pid_oom_kill_score'

watch-pid-oom-kill-score

Alias variant for follow-pid-oom-kill-score.

View Code 
alias watch-pid-oom-kill-score='follow_pid_oom_kill_score'

cat-pid-oom-adj-score

Displays the OOM adjustment score for a process from /proc/<pid>/oom_score_adj (range −1000 to 1000; lower = less likely to be killed).

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat_pid_oom_adj_score() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-adj-score <pid>"}"
  echo ==== "OOM SCORE (TO BE ADJUSTED)" ====
  sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || echo "No OOM adj info available"
}
alias cat-pid-oom-adj-score='cat_pid_oom_adj_score'

cat-pid-oom-scores

Displays both OOM kill score and adjustment score for a process in a single call.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat_pid_oom_scores() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-scores <pid>"}"
  echo ==== "OOM SCORE (TO BE KILLED)" ====
  sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score 2>/dev/null || echo "No OOM score info available"
  echo ==== "OOM SCORE (TO BE ADJUSTED)" ====
  sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || echo "No OOM adj info available"
}
alias cat-pid-oom-scores='cat_pid_oom_scores'
Usage Examples
$
$

journal-earlyoom

Follows the earlyoom systemd journal via sudo journalctl -u earlyoom .

View Code 
alias journal-earlyoom='sudo journalctl -u earlyoom'

journal-sysoomd

Follows the systemd-oomd journal via sudo journalctl -u systemd-oomd .

View Code 
alias journal-sysoomd='sudo journalctl -u systemd-oomd'

Pretty Output Aliases

Enhanced versions of system info commands with colorized output , headers, footers, and syntax highlighting.

cat-k-os-pretty

Displays the OS kernel version with header/footer decorations.

View Code 
cat-k-os-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Kernel OS Release"
  echo -e "  \033[1;32m►\033[0m $(sudo cat /proc/sys/kernel/osrelease 2>/dev/null)"
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-etc-os-pretty

Displays /etc/os-release with highlighted keys.

View Code 
cat-etc-os-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "OS Release Info — /etc/os-release"
  sudo cat /etc/os-release 2>/dev/null | sed \
    -e "s/^\([A-Z_]*\)=/\033[1;34m\1\033[0m=/" | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-all-env-vars-pretty

Pretty-prints all environment variables with header and numbered lines.

View Code 
show-all-env-vars-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "All Environment Variables (env)"
  env | grep -v '^$' | sort | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-all-printenv-vars-pretty

Pretty-prints all exported variables with header and numbered lines.

View Code 
show-all-printenv-vars-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "All Exported Variables (printenv)"
  printenv | grep -v '^$' | sort | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-all-sh-vars-pretty

Pretty-prints all shell variables with header and numbered lines.

View Code 
show-all-sh-vars-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "All Shell Variables (set)"
  ( set -o posix; set ) | grep -v '^$' | sort | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-ssh-service-pretty

Pretty-prints the SSH systemd service unit with header and numbered lines.

View Code 
cat-ssh-service-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "SSH Service Unit — /lib/systemd/system/ssh.service"
  sudo cat /lib/systemd/system/ssh.service 2>/dev/null | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

gted-pretty

Opens GNOME Text Editor with a pretty header message.

View Code 
gted-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "GNOME Text Editor"
  echo -e "  ► Opening gnome-text-editor…"
  gnome-text-editor "$@" &
  _pretty_ftr
}

uri-decode-pretty

Pretty-decodes a URI string showing input and decoded output with header.

View Code 
uri-decode-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "URI Decode"
  local result
  result=$(printf '%b' "${1//%/\\x}")
  echo -e "  Input:  $1"
  echo -e "  Output: $result"
  _pretty_ftr
}

printf-tr-pretty

Pretty-formats printf-tr result with header and error handling.

View Code 
printf-tr-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Printf with TR Substitution"
  local result
  result=$(printftr "$@" 2>&1)
  echo -e "  Result: $result"
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-indexed-pretty

Pretty-lists files by index with optional content display.

View Code 
cat-indexed-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Indexed File Listing"
  ls | nl -ba -w3 -s ' │ '
  _pretty_ftr
}

run-cmds-pretty

Pretty-runs multiple commands against a target with formatted output per command.

View Code 
run-cmds-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Run Multiple Commands"
  local target="$1"; shift
  for cmd in "$@"; do
    eval "$cmd \"$target\"" 2>/dev/null | _pretty_nl
  done
  _pretty_ftr
}

strip-html-comments-pretty

Pretty-strips HTML comments showing before/after count.

View Code 
strip-html-comments-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Strip HTML Comments & Prettier Format"
  strip_html_comments_format "$1"
  echo "  Stripped comments from $1"
  _pretty_ftr
}

extract-min-css-pretty

Pretty-extracts and minifies CSS with byte-size comparison.

View Code 
extract-min-css-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Extract & Minify CSS from HTML"
  extract_minify_css "$1" "$2" "$3"
  echo "  Extracted: $2 → Minified: $3"
  _pretty_ftr
}

count-html-comments-pretty

Pretty-counts HTML comments and lines in a file with formatted output.

View Code 
count-html-comments-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "HTML Comment & Line Count"
  echo "  Comments: $(grep -c '<!--' "$1")"
  echo "  Lines:    $(wc -l < "$1")"
  _pretty_ftr
}

check-css-minifier-pretty

Pretty-checks CSS minifier availability with checkmark/cross status.

View Code 
check-css-minifier-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "CSS Minifier Availability"
  npx csso --version 2>/dev/null && echo "  ✓ csso"
  npx clean-css-cli --version 2>/dev/null && echo "  ✓ clean-css-cli"
  _pretty_ftr
}

inject-min-css-pretty

Pretty-injects minified CSS into HTML with line count comparison.

View Code 
inject-min-css-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "Inject Minified CSS into HTML"
  inject_minified_css "$1" "$2"
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-oom-conf-pretty

Pretty-prints the systemd OOM daemon configuration with header/footer decorations.

View Code 
cat-oom-conf-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "OOM Daemon Config — /etc/systemd/oomd.conf"
  sudo cat /etc/systemd/oomd.conf 2>/dev/null | _pretty_nl || echo "No OOM config file found"
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-oom-kill-alloc-pretty

Pretty-prints vm.oom_kill_allocating_task sysctl value with an explanation label.

View Code 
show-oom-kill-alloc-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "OOM Kill Allocating Task"
  local val
  val=$(sudo sysctl vm.oom_kill_allocating_task 2>/dev/null)
  echo -e "  [1;33m${val}[0m  (0=kill random process 1=kill allocating task)"
  _pretty_ftr
}

follow-early-oom-pretty

Follows earlyoom output with a formatted header showing the interval.

function $1: interval (default: 2)
View Code 
follow-early-oom-pretty() {
  local interval="${1:-2}"
  _pretty_hdr "earlyoom — verbose reporting (interval: ${interval}s)"
  follow_early_oom_rec "$interval"
  _pretty_ftr
}

watch-early-oom-pretty

Alias variant for follow-early-oom-pretty.

View Code 
alias watch-early-oom-pretty='follow_early_oom_pretty'

watch-mem-hogs-pretty

Watches top RSS consumers with a formatted header.

function $1: interval (default: 0.25)
View Code 
watch-mem-hogs-pretty() {
  local interval="${1:-0.25}"
  _pretty_hdr "Memory Hogs — top RSS consumers (interval: ${interval}s)"
  watch_mem_hogs "$interval"
  _pretty_ftr
}

watch-cpu-hogs-pretty

Watches top CPU consumers with a formatted header.

function $1: interval (default: 0.25)
View Code 
watch-cpu-hogs-pretty() {
  local interval="${1:-0.25}"
  _pretty_hdr "CPU Hogs — top CPU consumers (interval: ${interval}s)"
  watch_cpu_hogs "$interval"
  _pretty_ftr
}

find-zombies-pretty

Lists zombie processes with a formatted header.

function $1: head (default: 20)
View Code 
find-zombies-pretty() {
  local head="${1:-20}"
  _pretty_hdr "Zombie Processes (state Z)"
  find_zombies "$head" | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-pid-oom-kill-score-pretty

Pretty-prints the OOM kill score for a process, with a label and explanation.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat-pid-oom-kill-score-pretty() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-kill-score-pretty <pid>"}"
  _pretty_hdr "OOM Kill Score — PID ${pid}"
  local val
  val=$(sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score 2>/dev/null)
  echo -e "  [1;33mOOM Score:[0m ${val:-N/A}  (higher = more likely to be killed)"
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-pid-oom-adj-score-pretty

Pretty-prints the OOM adjustment score for a process, with range explanation.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat-pid-oom-adj-score-pretty() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-adj-score-pretty <pid>"}"
  _pretty_hdr "OOM Adjustment Score — PID ${pid}"
  local val
  val=$(sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null)
  echo -e "  [1;33mOOM Score Adj:[0m ${val:-N/A}  (-1000=never kill  0=normal  1000=always kill)"
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-pid-oom-scores-pretty

Pretty-prints both OOM kill score and adjustment score for a process in one view.

function $1: pid (required)
View Code 
cat-pid-oom-scores-pretty() {
  local pid="${1?"Usage: cat-pid-oom-scores-pretty <pid>"}"
  _pretty_hdr "OOM Scores — PID ${pid}"
  local kill_score adj_score
  kill_score=$(sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score 2>/dev/null)
  adj_score=$(sudo cat /proc/"$pid"/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null)
  echo -e "  [1;33mOOM Score:[0m     ${kill_score:-N/A}  (higher = more likely to be killed)"
  echo -e "  [1;33mOOM Score Adj:[0m ${adj_score:-N/A}  (-1000=never kill  0=normal  1000=always kill)"
  _pretty_ftr
}

journal-earlyoom-pretty

Pretty-prints the earlyoom systemd journal with numbered lines and header/footer.

View Code 
journal-earlyoom-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "earlyoom Journal — systemd"
  sudo journalctl -u earlyoom 2>/dev/null | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

journal-sysoomd-pretty

Pretty-prints the systemd-oomd journal with numbered lines and header/footer.

View Code 
journal-sysoomd-pretty() {
  _pretty_hdr "systemd-oomd Journal"
  sudo journalctl -u systemd-oomd 2>/dev/null | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

Navigation Aliases

Quick shortcuts for directory navigation.

desk

cd ~/Desktop

View Code 
alias desk='cd ~/Desktop'
Usage Examples
$

docs

cd ~/Documents

View Code 
alias docs='cd ~/Documents'
Usage Examples
$

..

Go up one directory level

View Code 
alias ..='cd ..'

Aliases Laravel / PHP

Artisan commands, migrations, and Laravel development shortcuts.

artclrs

Full Laravel reset: clears all caches, removes bootstrap cache files, runs dump-autoload, resets & re-seeds migrations, lists routes, and starts the server.

destructive
View Code 
alias artclrs='
php artisan permission:cache-reset; \
php artisan config:clear; \
php artisan cache:clear; \
php artisan optimize:clear; \
php artisan route:clear; \
php artisan view:clear; \
php artisan clear-compiled; \
rm -f bootstrap/cache/services.php bootstrap/cache/packages.php bootstrap/cache/compiled.php bootstrap/cache/routes.php; \
composer dump-autoload -o; \
php artisan migrate:status; \
php artisan migrate:reset; \
php artisan migrate:fresh --seed; \
php artisan route:list --sort=uri; \
php artisan serve
'
Usage Examples
$

HTML & CSS Tools

Workflow utilities for stripping HTML comments, extracting and minifying CSS, and injecting optimized stylesheets.

strip-html-comments

Strips all HTML comments from a file using a non-greedy Perl regex, then reformats with Prettier (HTML parser, 120 print-width, single-attribute-per-line).

function $1: path
View Code 
strip_html_comments_format() {
  local path="${1:?Usage: strip-html-comments <html_file>}"
  [[ -f "$path" ]] || { echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mFile not found:\033[0m $path"; return 1; }
  perl -0777 -i -pe 's/<!--(?!.*?--\s*>.*?<!--).*?-->//gs' "$path" \
    && npx prettier --parser html --print-width 120 --tab-width 2 \
         --no-semi --single-attribute-per-line \
         --html-whitespace-sensitivity ignore --write "$path"
}
alias strip-html-comments='strip_html_comments_format'
Usage Examples
$

extract-min-css

Extracts the <style> block from an HTML file, minifies it with clean-css-cli , and displays original vs minified byte sizes.

function $1: src $2: extracted.css $3: minified.css
View Code 
extract_minify_css() {
  local src="${1:?Usage: extract-min-css <html> <extracted.css> <minified.css>}"
  local extract_src="${2:?}" extract_min="${3:?}"
  [[ -f "$src" ]] || { echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mSource not found:\033[0m $src"; return 1; }
  perl -0777 -ne '/<style[^>]*>(.*?)<\/style>/s && print $1' "$src" > "$extract_src" \
    && npx clean-css-cli -o "$extract_min" "$extract_src" \
    && wc -c "$extract_src" "$extract_min"
}
alias extract-min-css='extract_minify_css'
Usage Examples
$

count-html-comments

Counts HTML comments and total lines in a file.

function $1: path
View Code 
count_html_comments() {
  local path="${1:?Usage: count-html-comments <html_file>}"
  [[ -f "$path" ]] || { echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mFile not found:\033[0m $path"; return 1; }
  echo "Comments: $(grep -c '<!--' "$path")"
  echo "Lines:    $(wc -l < "$path")"
}
alias count-html-comments='count_html_comments'
Usage Examples
$

check-css-minifier

Checks which CSS minifier CLI is available: csso or clean-css-cli . Prints the version or "NONE".

function
View Code 
check_css_minifier() {
  npx csso --version 2>/dev/null \
    || npx clean-css-cli --version 2>/dev/null \
    || echo "NONE"
}
alias check-css-minifier='check_css_minifier'
Usage Examples
$

inject-min-css

Injects a minified CSS file into the <style> block of an HTML file, then restores @media and @container at-rules that were collapsed during minification.

function $1: html_file $2: minified.css
View Code 
inject_minified_css() {
  local src="${1:?Usage: inject-min-css <html_file> <minified_css_file>}"
  local min_css="${2:?}"
  [[ -f "$src" ]] || { echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mSource not found:\033[0m $src"; return 1; }
  [[ -f "$min_css" ]] || { echo -e "❌ \033[1;31mCSS not found:\033[0m $min_css"; return 1; }
  local css
  css=$(<"$min_css")
  perl -0777 -i -pe \
    "s{(<style[^>]*>).*?(</style>)}{\$1${css}\$2}s" "$src"
  perl -0777 -i -pe '\n    s/\}\s*(print|screen|all|speech)\s*\{/}\@media $1\{/g;\n    s/(container-type:\s*[a-z-]+\s*\})\s*(\([^)]+\)\s*\{)/$1\@container $2/g;\n    s/\}\s+(\([^)]+\)\s*\{)/}\@media $1/g;\n  ' "$src"
  echo -e "✅ Injected into \033[1m$src\033[0m ($(wc -l < "$src") lines)"
}
alias inject-min-css='inject_minified_css'
Usage Examples
$

🚀 More aliases available in the full reference

This documentation covers a selection of the most commonly used aliases. The full .bashrc.pub.sh file contains 200+ aliases and functions organized into categories.

View Full Source

Desktop Environment

Aliases related to desktop sessions, display servers, and window managers.

See System Setup above for display and session aliases.

get-all-icons

List ALL icons in the system (user, legacy, system-wide, snap, flatpak, pixmaps).

View Code 
get_all_icons() {
  printf "\n[+] USER SPECIFIC ICONS\n"
  find ~/.local/share/icons/ -type f ... 
  printf "\n[+] SYSTEM-WIDE ICONS\n"
  sudo find /usr/share/icons/ -type f ...
  # (also checks snap, flatpak, pixmaps)
}
alias get-all-icons='get_all_icons'

ls-all-icons

Alias for get-all-icons.

View Code 
alias ls-all-icons='get_all_icons'

Displays user mimeapps

View Code

Show systemd system.conf configuration.

View Code

cat-sysctl-conf function

Display /etc/sysctl.conf and all files in /etc/sysctl.d/.

View Code 
cat_sysctl_conf() {
  printf "[*] Checking /etc/sysctl.conf...\n"
  sudo cat /etc/sysctl.conf 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No sysctl.conf file found\n"
  sleep 2
  printf "[*] Checking /etc/sysctl.d/ directory for additional config files...\n"
  sudo find /etc/sysctl.d/ -type f -exec sh -c 'printf "[=== %s ===]\n" "$1"; sleep 1; cat "$1" 2>/dev/null' _ {} \;
}

ls-sysctl-conf

Alias for cat-sysctl-conf.

View Code 
alias ls-sysctl-conf='cat_sysctl_conf'

show-sysctl-conf

Alias for cat-sysctl-conf.

View Code 
alias show-sysctl-conf='cat_sysctl_conf'

cat-sysctl-services function

Display systemd-sysctl.service and sysinit.target.wants sysctl overrides.

View Code 
cat_systemd_sysctl_services() {
  printf "[*] Checking systemd-sysctl.service for sysctl overrides...\n"
  sudo cat /usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-sysctl.service 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No systemd-sysctl.service file found\n"
  sleep 1
  printf "[*] Checking systemd services for sysctl overrides...\n"
  sleep 2
  sudo cat /usr/lib/systemd/system/sysinit.target.wants/systemd-sysctl.service 2>/dev/null || printf "[-] No sysctl overrides found in systemd service files\n"
}

ls-sysctl-services

Alias for cat-sysctl-services.

View Code 
alias ls-sysctl-services='cat_systemd_sysctl_services'

show-sysctl-services

Alias for cat-sysctl-services.

View Code 
alias show-sysctl-services='cat_systemd_sysctl_services'

list-paths-no-vendors

Finds and sorts files by path length, excluding node_modules, vendor, and backup directories.

function $1: extension (required) $2: src (default: .) $3: max_depth (default: 15) $4: cut_indexes (default: 0)
View Code 
list_paths_no_vendors() {
  local extension=${1?Usage: list-paths-no-vendors <extension> ?<src=.> ?<max_depth=15> ?<cut_indexes=0>}
  local src=${2:-.}
  local max_depth=${3:-15}
  local cut_indexes=${4:-0}
  local sorted_res=$(find "$src" -maxdepth "$max_depth" -type f \( -not -path "*node_modules*" -not -path "*/vendor/*" -not -path "*backup/*" -not -path "*.venv/" -path "*${extension}" \) | awk '{print length,$0}' | sort -n -k1,1 -k2)
  if (( ! cut_indexes == 0 )); then
    sorted_res=$(echo "$sorted_res" | cut -d' ' -f2-)
  fi
  echo "$sorted_res"
}
alias list-paths-no-vendors='list_paths_no_vendors'
Usage Examples
$

PowerShell Equivalents

Cross-platform PowerShell equivalents. See Profile.pub.ps1 for the full profile.

PowerShell equivalents use Set-Alias and advanced functions.

XFCE Configuration

XFCE and desktop environment configuration aliases.

Desktop config aliases like cat-kde-settings, cat-gdm3-conf read DE-specific configuration.

show-libvirt-conf alias

Show libvirt daemon configuration from /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf.

View Code 
alias show-libvirt-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

ls-libvirt-conf alias

Alias for show-libvirt-conf.

View Code 
alias ls-libvirt-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

cat-libvirt-conf alias

Alias for show-libvirt-conf.

View Code 
alias cat-libvirt-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

show-libv-conf alias

Alias for show-libvirt-conf (short form).

View Code 
alias show-libv-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

ls-libv-conf alias

Alias for show-libvirt-conf (short form).

View Code 
alias ls-libv-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

cat-libv-conf alias

Alias for show-libvirt-conf (short form).

View Code 
alias cat-libv-conf='sudo cat /etc/libvirt/libvirtd.conf'

⌨️ Bash Aliases Reference | Last updated: March 13, 2026

PowerShell

Skip to main content
PS Profile
  • System
  • Git
  • Pretty
  • Utilities
  • HTML/CSS
SECTIONS System Setup Basic Commands File Analysis Git Aliases Pretty Output Navigation Aliases Laravel/PHP Aliases HTML/CSS Tools

PowerShell Profile Reference

Comprehensive documentation of PowerShell functions and aliases transpiled from Bash for cross-platform workflows.

102+ Aliases Syntax Highlighted Collapsible Code

System Setup

Functions for querying environment variables, user info, and shell metadata.

show-all-env-vars

List all environment variables sorted alphabetically.

function
View Code 
function Show-AllEnvVars {
  Get-ChildItem Env: | Sort-Object Name | Format-Table -AutoSize
}
Usage Examples
PS>

show-all-printenv-vars

List all exported variables sorted alphabetically.

function
View Code 
function Show-AllPrintenvVars {
  [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariables() |
    ForEach-Object { $_.GetEnumerator() } |
    Sort-Object Key | Format-Table Key, Value -AutoSize
}

show-all-sh-vars

List all PowerShell variables sorted alphabetically.

function
View Code 
function Show-AllShVars {
  Get-Variable | Sort-Object Name | Format-Table Name, Value -AutoSize
}

show-hosttype

Show host machine architecture.

function
View Code 
function Show-Hosttype {
  [System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation]::OSArchitecture
}
Usage Examples
PS>

show-home

Show current user's home directory path.

function
View Code 
function Show-Home { $HOME }
Usage Examples
PS>

show-user

Show current username.

function
View Code 
function Show-User { $env:USERNAME ?? $env:USER }
Usage Examples
PS>

show-shell

Show current PowerShell version and edition.

function
View Code 
function Show-Shell {
  "PowerShell $($PSVersionTable.PSVersion) ($($PSVersionTable.PSEdition))"
}
Usage Examples
PS>

show-wrkdir

Show current working directory.

function
View Code 
function Show-Wrkdir { $PWD.Path }
Usage Examples
PS>

schedule-kill-sequence

Schedule a process kill at a specified time.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-ScheduleKillSequence { ... }
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>

set-ps-critical

Set OOM score adjustment for a process to critical (-1000).

function
View Code 
function Set-PsCritical { ... }
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>

Basic Commands

Utility functions for file listing, URI decoding, string formatting, and more.

mkdir-p

Shortcut for mkdir (New-Item -ItemType Directory).

function
View Code 
function mkd {
  param([string]$Path)
  New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $Path -Force
}
Usage Examples
PS>

uri-decode

Decode a percent-encoded URI string.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-UriDecode {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Uri)
  [System.Uri]::UnescapeDataString($Uri)
}
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>

printf-tr

Printf with field-width, delimiter, and regex substitution.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-PrintfTr {
  param(
    [string]$Delimiter = '',
    [int]$Width = 0,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Pattern,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Substitute
  )
  $result = $Target -replace [regex]::Escape($Pattern), $Substitute
  # padding logic...
  $result
}
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>
PS>

cat-indexed

List files with index numbers and display file contents by index.

function
View Code 
function Show-CatIndexed {
  param([int]$Index = 1)
  $files = Get-ChildItem -File | Sort-Object Name
  foreach ($f in $files) {
    Write-Host ("{0,4} │ {1}" -f $i, $f.Name)
  }
}
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>

run-cmds

Run multiple script blocks against a single target argument.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-RunCmds {
  param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Target,
    [Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][scriptblock[]]$Commands
  )
  foreach ($cmd in $Commands) { & $cmd $Target }
}
Usage Examples
PS>
PS>

ls-lah-859

Custom ls output showing time, size, and name columns.

function
View Code 
function Show-LsLah859 {
  param([string]$Path = '.')
  Get-ChildItem -Path $Path |
    Format-Table Time, Size, Name -AutoSize
}

wc-l-novendors

Count total lines in directory excluding vendor folders.

function
View Code 
function Measure-LinesNoVendors {
  $exclude = @('vendor','node_modules','.git','dist','build')
  Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File |
    Where-Object { # filter excluded dirs } |
    ForEach-Object { # count lines }
}
Usage Examples
PS>

ls-web-images

Find common web image formats in a directory.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name ls-web-images -Value Find-WebImages

show-web-images

Alias for ls-web-images.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name show-web-images -Value Find-WebImages

ls-all-images

Find a broad set of image formats (web + RAW + design files).

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name ls-all-images -Value Find-AllImages

show-all-images

Alias for ls-all-images.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name show-all-images -Value Find-AllImages

ls-web-images-deep

Find common web image formats with depth controls.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name ls-web-images-deep -Value Find-WebImagesDeep

show-web-images-deep

Alias for ls-web-images-deep.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name show-web-images-deep -Value Find-WebImagesDeep

ls-all-images-deep

Find a broad set of image formats with depth controls.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name ls-all-images-deep -Value Find-AllImagesDeep

show-all-images-deep

Alias for ls-all-images-deep.

simple alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name show-all-images-deep -Value Find-AllImagesDeep

calculate-check-sum

Calculate Modulus N check digits for a numeric string (e.g. CPF mod-11, CNPJ).

function
View Code 
function Get-CheckSum {
  [CmdletBinding()]
  param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$State,
    [Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$Total
  )
  if ($State -notmatch '^\d+$') { Write-Error "State must be a numeric string."; return }
  if ($Total -lt 2) { Write-Error "Total must be an integer >= 2."; return }
  $stateLen = $State.Length
  $diff = $Total - $stateLen
  if ($diff -lt 1) { Write-Error "Total must be greater than the length of State."; return }
  for ($pos = 1; $pos -le $diff; $pos++) {
    $curLen = $stateLen + $pos - 1
    $sr = 0
    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $curLen; $i++) {
      $digit = [int]::Parse($State[$i].ToString())
      $weight = $stateLen + $pos - $i
      $sr += $digit * $weight
    }
    $rest = $sr % $Total
    $checkDigit = if ($rest -lt 2) { 0 } else { $Total - $rest }
    $State += $checkDigit.ToString()
  }
  $State
}
Usage Examples
PS>

cdup

Change directory up N levels using dots or .{N}.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-CdUp {
  [CmdletBinding()]
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Dots)
  $usage = "Usage: cdup .... | cdup .{N}"
  if ($Dots -match '^\.+$') { $n = $Dots.Length }
  elseif ($Dots -match '^\.\{(\d+)\}$') { $n = [int]$Matches[1] }
  else { Write-Error $usage; return }
  if ($n -lt 1) { Write-Error "N must be >= 1"; return }
  $sep = [System.IO.Path]::DirectorySeparatorChar
  $path = (@('..') * $n) -join $sep
  Set-Location -Path $path
}
Usage Examples
PS>

File Analysis

Functions for analyzing file content and directory structure.

is-mblank

Check if a file has multiple consecutive blank lines.

function
View Code 
function Test-MultipleBlankLines {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$File)
  foreach ($line in Get-Content $File) {
    if ($line -match '^\s*$') { $blank++ }
    else { $blank = 0 }
  }
}
Usage Examples
PS>

ls-mblank

List files with multiple consecutive blank lines.

function
View Code 
function Show-MultipleBlankLinesFiles {
  Get-ChildItem -File | ForEach-Object {
    # scan each file for consecutive blank lines
  }
}
Usage Examples
PS>

list-files

List files with name, path, and size.

function
View Code 
function Show-ListFilesDetail {
  Get-ChildItem -Recurse -File | ForEach-Object {
    "NAME: $($_.Name) | PATH: $($_.FullName) | SIZE: $($_.Length)"
  }
}

contains-files

Check which directories contain files.

function
View Code 
function Show-ContainsFiles {
  param([switch]$Recurse)
  Get-ChildItem -Directory | Where-Object {
    (Get-ChildItem $_.FullName -File | Select-Object -First 1) -ne $null
  }
}

packf

Pack all files into subdirectories of 100 files each.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-PackFiles {
  $files = Get-ChildItem -File
  foreach ($f in $files) {
    Move-Item $f.FullName -Destination $dir
  }
}

fully-randomized-file-names

Rename all files in the current directory to random 16-char alphanumeric names, preserving extensions.

function
View Code 
function Rename-FilesRandomly {
  Get-ChildItem -File | ForEach-Object {
    $ext = $_.Extension
    $newName = -join ((48..57) + (65..90) + (97..122) | Get-Random -Count 16 | ForEach-Object { [char]$_ })
    Rename-Item $_.FullName -NewName "$newName$ext"
  }
}
Usage Examples
PS>

randomize-filenames

Alias for Rename-FilesRandomly.

alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name randomize-filenames -Value Rename-FilesRandomly

rand-fn

Alias for Rename-FilesRandomly (short form).

alias
View Code 
Set-Alias -Name rand-fn -Value Rename-FilesRandomly

get-mimeapps

Displays the user's default applications list.

function
View Code 
function get-mimeapps { Get-Content ~/.config/mimeapps.list -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
Usage Examples
PS>

get-share-mimeapps

Displays shared mimeapps list.

function
View Code 
function get-share-mimeapps { Get-Content ~/.local/share/applications/mimeapps.list -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }

get-all-mimeapps

Displays all mimeapps lists.

function
View Code 
function get-all-mimeapps { get-mimeapps; get-share-mimeapps }
Usage Examples
PS>

get-compose-chars

Displays X11 Compose keys.

function
View Code 
function get-compose-chars { Get-Content /usr/share/X11/locale/en_US.UTF-8/Compose -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
Usage Examples
PS>

show-compose-chars

Alias for get-compose-chars.

alias
View Code 
Set-Alias show-compose-chars get-compose-chars
Usage Examples
PS>

Git Aliases

Git command shortcuts for everyday version control workflows.

gra

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Gra { git remote add @args }

git remote add origin

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-GraO { git remote add origin @args }

ga

git add

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Ga { git add @args }
Usage Examples
PS>

gal

git add .

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Gal { git add . }

gc

git commit

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Gc { git commit @args }
Usage Examples
PS>

gca

git commit -a -m

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Gca { git commit -a -m @args }

galps

cd to repo root, git add . && git commit -am

function
View Code 
function Invoke-Galps {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Message)
  $top = git rev-parse --show-toplevel
  if ($top) { Set-Location $top }
  git add .
  git commit -am $Message
}

gl

git log

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Gl { git log @args }

gl-o

git log --oneline

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-GlO { git log --oneline @args }

View Code

Pretty Output

Pretty-formatted versions of functions with headers, footers, and colored output.

show-all-env-vars-pretty

Pretty-print all environment variables with header.

pretty
View Code 
function Show-AllEnvVarsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "All Environment Variables"
  Get-ChildItem Env: | Sort-Object Name |
    ForEach-Object { "$($_.Name)=$($_.Value)" } | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-all-printenv-vars-pretty

Pretty-print all exported variables.

pretty
View Code 
function Show-AllPrintenvVarsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "All Exported Variables"
  [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariables().GetEnumerator() |
    Sort-Object Key |
    ForEach-Object { "$($_.Key)=$($_.Value)" } | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

show-all-sh-vars-pretty

Pretty-print all PowerShell variables.

pretty
View Code 
function Show-AllShVarsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "All PowerShell Variables"
  Get-Variable | Sort-Object Name |
    ForEach-Object { "$($_.Name) = $($_.Value)" } | _pretty_nl
  _pretty_ftr
}

uri-decode-pretty

Pretty-decode a URI string showing input and decoded output.

pretty
View Code 
function Invoke-UriDecodePretty {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Uri)
  _pretty_hdr "URI Decode"
  Write-Host "  Input:  $Uri" -ForegroundColor Blue
  Write-Host "  Output: $([System.Uri]::UnescapeDataString($Uri))" -ForegroundColor Green
  _pretty_ftr
}

cat-indexed-pretty

Pretty-list files by index with optional content display.

pretty
View Code 
function Show-CatIndexedPretty {
  param([int]$Index = 0)
  _pretty_hdr "Indexed File Listing"
  # lists files, optionally shows content
  _pretty_ftr
}

run-cmds-pretty

Pretty-run multiple commands with formatted output.

pretty
View Code 
function Invoke-RunCmdsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "Run Multiple Commands"
  Write-Host "  Target: $Target" -ForegroundColor Blue
  foreach ($cmd in $Commands) { & $cmd $Target | _pretty_nl }
  _pretty_ftr
}

printf-tr-pretty

Pretty-print printf-tr result with formatted header.

pretty
View Code 
function Invoke-PrintfTrPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "Printf with TR Substitution"
  $result = Invoke-PrintfTr @PSBoundParameters
  Write-Host "  Result: $result" -ForegroundColor Green
  _pretty_ftr
}

strip-html-comments-pretty

Pretty-strip HTML comments showing before/after count.

pretty
View Code 
function Remove-HtmlCommentsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "Strip HTML Comments"
  # counts before, runs Remove-HtmlComments, counts after
  _pretty_ftr
}

extract-min-css-pretty

Pretty-extract and minify CSS with byte-size comparison.

pretty
View Code 
function Export-MinifyCssPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "Extract & Minify CSS"
  Export-MinifyCss -Src $Src -ExtractDst $ExtractDst -MinDst $MinDst
  _pretty_ftr
}

count-html-comments-pretty

Pretty-count HTML comments and lines.

pretty
View Code 
function Measure-HtmlCommentsPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "HTML Comment & Line Count"
  Write-Host "  Comments: $comments"
  Write-Host "  Lines: $lines"
  _pretty_ftr
}

check-css-minifier-pretty

Pretty-check CSS minifier availability.

pretty
View Code 
function Test-CssMinifierPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "CSS Minifier Availability"
  # tests csso and clean-css-cli with ✓/✗ icons
  _pretty_ftr
}

inject-min-css-pretty

Pretty-inject minified CSS into HTML.

pretty
View Code 
function Set-MinifiedCssPretty {
  _pretty_hdr "Inject Minified CSS"
  Set-MinifiedCss -Src $Src -MinCss $MinCss
  _pretty_ftr
}

alias
View Code

artcl

Clear all Laravel caches.

function
View Code 
function Invoke-Artcl {
  php artisan permission:cache-reset
  php artisan config:clear
  php artisan cache:clear
  php artisan optimize:clear
  php artisan route:clear
  php artisan view:clear
  php artisan clear-compiled
}

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Artsv { php artisan serve }

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Artrtl { php artisan route:list --sort=uri }

laravel-rm-cache

Remove Laravel bootstrap cache files.

function
View Code 
function Remove-LaravelCache {
  $cache = @('bootstrap/cache/services.php', ...)
  foreach ($f in $cache) { Remove-Item $f -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
}

compdp

composer dump-autoload -o

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Compdp { composer dump-autoload -o }

mysqlr

MySQL login as root with password prompt.

alias
View Code 
function Invoke-Mysqlr { mysql -u root -p }

HTML/CSS Tools

Functions for HTML comment stripping, CSS extraction, minification, and injection.

strip-html-comments

Strip all HTML comments from a file and reformat with Prettier.

function
View Code 
function Remove-HtmlComments {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path)
  $content = Get-Content $Path -Raw
  $content = [regex]::Replace($content, '<!--.*?-->', '')
  Set-Content -Path $Path -Value $content
  npx prettier --write $Path
}
Usage Examples
PS>

extract-min-css

Extract style content from HTML, minify with clean-css-cli.

function
View Code 
function Export-MinifyCss {
  param(
    [string]$Src,
    [string]$ExtractDst,
    [string]$MinDst
  )
  # extract <style> content, minify via npx clean-css-cli
}
Usage Examples
PS>

count-html-comments

Count HTML comments and total lines in a file.

function
View Code 
function Measure-HtmlComments {
  param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path)
  $comments = (Get-Content $Path | Select-String '<!--').Count
  $lines = (Get-Content $Path).Count
}
Usage Examples
PS>

check-css-minifier

Check which CSS minifier CLI is available.

function
View Code 
function Test-CssMinifier {
  npx csso --version
  npx clean-css-cli --version
}
Usage Examples
PS>

inject-min-css

Inject a minified CSS file into the style block of an HTML file.

function
View Code 
function Set-MinifiedCss {
  param(
    [string]$Src,
    [string]$MinCss
  )
  $css = Get-Content $MinCss -Raw
  $html = Get-Content $Src -Raw
  $html = [regex]::Replace($html, '(?s)(<style[^>]*>).*?(</style>)', "$1$css$2")
  Set-Content -Path $Src -Value $html
}
Usage Examples
PS>

About This Reference

This page documents all publicable PowerShell profile functions and aliases, transpiled from the Bash publicable section. Linux-only commands have been excluded.
